text: The CMCS shall provide the physical link between the WIDAR Correlator hardware and the EVLA monitor and control system.
The MCCC shall receive configuration information from the EVLA M&C system.
The MCCC shall translate EVLA M&C configuration information into a physical correlator hardware configuration.
The CMCS shall process and transfer dynamic control data including models and filter parameters.
The CMCS shall monitor correlator subsystem health.
The CMCS shall take autonomous corrective action to recover from hardware and computing system faults.
The CMCS shall provide tools to collect and display auto correlation products.
The CMCS shall provide system wide access to all correlator system states.
The CMCS shall provide error and status messages in a concise time and location referenced format.
The MCCC shall accept external data feeds for models and time standards.
The CMCS shall issue an alert notice if a CMIB subsystem fails to respond to reboot requests.
The CPCC shall activate the backup MCCC system automatically upon non-recoverable failure of the primary MCCC.
The interface between the CMIB and MCCC shall be Ethernet compliant with IEEE 802.3.
The CMCS network data rate shall be 100 Mbits/sec or better.
The MCCC-CMIB and MCCC-EVLA M&C networks shall reside on separate physical interfaces.
There shall be a redundant serial RS-232c communication path between the MCCC and CPCC.
The CMIB shall read a 16-bit identifier from the host correlator board to form a unique IP address.
The CMIB shall read the contents of all writeable hardware control registers.
The CMIB shall execute hardware warm boots upon external command from the MCCC.
The CMIB carrier board shall provide an externally visible LED indicating operational status.
The CMCS computers shall be powered through UPS devices with capacity for coordinated system shutdown.
Each computer system in the CMCS shall have a hardware-based watchdog timer to trigger a reboot upon system hang.
The CMIB shall conform to PC104+ electrical and physical standards.
The CMIB shall contain 64 Mbytes or greater of SDRAM.
The MCCC shall be a high availability general-purpose computer supporting multiple Ethernet interfaces.
The MCCC shall have local disk and file systems to allow standalone operation without external communication.
The CMCS processors shall meet all data processing deadlines.
All system error and debug messages shall be present at the MCCC layer.
All messages passed between CMCS layers shall include UTC and wall clock time stamps.
The CMCS shall provide a Graphical User Interface for remote testing and debugging through the VCI.
The CMCS shall detect and report operating system crashes and temperature deviations.
All users of the CMCS shall login using unique identification and encrypted passwords.
The system administrator shall have unrestricted access to all aspects of the system.
The CMCS shall maintain operations on unaffected resources during partial shutdowns for maintenance.
Software system code shall be written in no more than two generally familiar languages.
